cards.title: AI Fatigue is Real: Builders are Drowning in Bad AI Output and Context Switching
cards.thesis: Builders are increasingly frustrated with the quality and workflow of current AI tools, like Claude Code, leading to 'exhaustion,' 'subtle bugs,' and a fear of becoming 'lazier.' While AI promises speed, the reality for many solo founders and vibe coders is a struggle with context switching and output that's 'horrible at writing,' forcing them to question if building a solo business with AI is even possible.

evidence.text: Many developers using AI as a daily driver, like with Claude Code, feel 'exhausted by all the context switching from managing 2-3 agents at a time,' noting that letting agents run wild leads to 'subtle bugs that compound.'

One user found that Opus 4.7, especially when used in Claude Code, was 'horrible at writing,' describing its output as 'sloppy, unprecise, very empty sentences,' a stark contrast to previous versions.

People are getting 'depressed day by day about what is coming' with AI, worrying that companies won't 'tolerate careful work when others can deliver same with 10% less quality but 60% faster,' leading to an internal fight about their own efforts.

A common sentiment among engineers is that 'building a solo business is impossible,' as they 'fell into the "build, and they will come" trap' and can't find traction, despite trying to build, calibrate, and engage with customers.
Some users have noticed 'AI tools have made me lazier,' replacing deep thinking and reasoning with conversations with AI, leading to concerns about not exercising their 'deep thinking muscles much.'

cards.opportunity: Everyone's trying to make AI *generate more*, but builders using tools like Claude Code are getting burned by *quality* and *workflow* issues that lead to 'subtle bugs' and 'exhaustion.' The opportunity isn't more generative AI, but a lightweight 'AI workflow co-pilot' that acts like a smart project manager or 'AI peer reviewer,' automatically catching 'sloppy' output or offering context-aware suggestions to reduce 'context switching' across different AI agents. Ship it as a plugin for popular dev environments like Cursor or Replit, and you'll own the market of 'depressed' but ambitious builders struggling with AI fatigue.
